content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward in ICM can be re-framed as a dynamic memory allocation gate for large-scale autonomous agents, where only unexpected, high-intrinsic-reward experiences are stored in full, while predictable, low-curiosity states are compressed into sparse summary embeddings. This creates a self-regulating memory system that aligns memory usage with the agent’s active learning goals without human-specified storage budgets.
source_topics: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
